Notice Of Renewed Motion To Compel Compliance With Third-Party Subpoena And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jul-16-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Ryan Pinkston, Esq. and Bailey Bifoss, Esq. 415-397-2823 bbifoss@seyfarth.comfor Plaintiff.
Joshua Koltun, Esq. 415-680-3410for Defendant Doe 4.
Bill Frimmel, Esq. 650-322-3048 bill@sffwlaw.com for Third Party Glass Door.

?THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF SYNAPSE FINANCIAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC., SANKAET PATHAK's Motion To Compel Compliance With Third-Party Subpoena And Production Of Documents.

Motion To Compel Compliance With Third-Party Subpoena And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jan-17-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Ryan Pinkston, Esq. of SEYFARTH SHAW LLP, T:415.397.2823, for plaintiff/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs Synapse Financial Technologies, Inc. and Sankaet Pathak's "motion to compel compliance with third-party subpoena and production of documents" from Glassdoor, Inc. is denied without prejudice. I adopt the judge pro tem's thorough and well-reasoned report and recommendation. Glassdoor cites no authority for its notion that a California court in a county where a libel suit is filed can be required to transfer discovery disputes in that suit to another county's court. Nor is a libel plaintiff required to first pursue alternative means to subpoenaing a website for posters' identities; rather, constitutional interests are protected by a plaintiff's prima facie showing of defamation. (Krinsky v. Doe 6 (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 1154.) Plaintiffs may file a motion to compel making a more robust showing that the statements set out on page 12 of the judge pro tem's report are defamatory.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and attaches the judge pro tem's report.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
